feed.title: Combined summary - BIP Proposal: Stateless PSBT Coordination (Blind Relay)

entry.title: Combined summary - BIP Proposal: Stateless PSBT Coordination (Blind Relay)
entry.summary: The development of a Stateless PSBT Coordination protocol by Sean Carlin addresses the need for secure multisig transaction coordination across different hardware and software wallets without depending on centralized servers that potentially expose user data. The proposed "Blind Relay" model, detailed in a draft BIP [(bip-stateless-psbt-coordination)](https://github.com/scarlin90/bip-stateless-psbt-coordination), employs an ephemeral WebSocket architecture ensuring end-to-end encryption. Importantly, the relay server remains ignorant of the PSBT, xpubs, or signatures involved, enhancing privacy and security. This stateless approach ensures that all data related to the transaction is erased either when the room is closed or after 24 hours from creation. Further developments include the release of [Signing Room v1.8.0](https://github.com/scarlin90/signingroom/releases/tag/v1.8.0) and later v2.0.0, which introduced significant improvements including a simple web component integration feature through a single HTML tag, as detailed in the [full release notes](https://github.com/scarlin90/signingroom/releases/tag/v2.0.0). These versions focus on operational security by separating the transmission of room links and decryption keys within the user interface, thereby reducing the risk of interception or unauthorized access. Additionally, the launch of `@signing-room/sdk` v1.0.0 marks a pivotal enhancement in the framework's capabilities. With the SDK, developers can now integrate the core cryptographic functions and coordination logic into diverse applications beyond the main Angular client. This TypeScript-based SDK supports real-time synchronization of room creations, participant joins, and signature uploads, facilitating seamless PSBT coordination. The SDK also features a developer walkthrough that explains the implementation process and showcases its flexibility in creating secure transaction environments programmatically. This includes generating cryptographic seals for transaction integrity and enabling dynamic, event-driven multisig ceremonies. Interested parties are encouraged to review these innovations and provide feedback on API usability and additional desired features, available through the SDK's repository on [GitHub](https://github.com/scarlin90/signingroom/tree/main/libs/sdk) and its [NPM package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@signing-room/sdk?activeTab=readme). The corresponding video walkthrough can be accessed [here](https://youtu.be/yzcFlK6c6t0).
